acceptance_targets: [chapter_03_award_01] PASS if the revised dinner scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:245-377 contains at least one moment where Lena believes Eli might lose the room, another character follows that wobble with a real test or reservation, and the later invitation/probation beat lands after that recovery. FAIL if the call remains an uninterrupted run of successful jokes and instant buy-in.

[chapter_03_craft_01] PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:497-571 progresses in distinct steps from social proof to self-revelation to future risk, and no two adjacent Lena speeches make the same 'he fit in the room / they liked him' point in near-paraphrase. FAIL if the scene still restates public approval multiple times before moving on.

[chapter_03_prose_01] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 contain no more than one narrator-side 'That was...' or 'It was...' summary opener across those beats, and each moment now lands through specific action, sensation, or social consequence. FAIL if multiple abstract deictic labels remain in those same pivots.

[MEDIUM_CH03_IDIOLECT_COLLISION] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:997-1010 still lands the joke but Gabe's line could not be reassigned to June or Eli without sounding wrong, and the exact phrase 'I contain multitudes' no longer appears in this chapter. Fail if Gabe still speaks in the same mantra line as the two voices the plot later wants linked.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised dinner scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:245-377 contains at least one moment where Lena believes Eli might lose the room, another character follows that wobble with a real test or reservation, and the later invitation/probation beat lands after that recovery. FAIL if the call remains an uninterrupted run of successful jokes and instant buy-in.
findings.problem: The chapter's defining public-legitimation set piece is too frictionless to feel shortlist-level. Once Eli joins the table, scrutiny converts to approval almost without a wobble, so the scene proves that he charms the family but does not make Lena or the room pay enough social risk on the way there.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the dinner-call spine at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:245, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:315, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:341 so Eli has to survive at least one concrete snag before Gabe and the table warm to him. Options: let Gabe press on work specifics, let Mateo catch a too-slick answer, or let one joke miss and force Eli to recalibrate. Keep the eventual acceptance, but make it feel earned rather than pre-cleared.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:497-571 progresses in distinct steps from social proof to self-revelation to future risk, and no two adjacent Lena speeches make the same 'he fit in the room / they liked him' point in near-paraphrase. FAIL if the scene still restates public approval multiple times before moving on.
findings.problem: The sidewalk aftermath slightly over-explains what the dinner already dramatized. Lena repeats the same validation point in adjacent turns: they heard him, Gabe did not brace, the girls liked him, Mateo liked him, Marisol liked him, he fit the room. The emotional logic is true, but the scene circles it instead of escalating cleanly from public approval to the deeper danger of belief.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten and differentiate the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:497, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:513, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:517, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:561 so each Lena speech does a different job. For example: first name Gabe's changed posture, then pivot quickly to the uglier admission that family approval makes Eli feel less invented and therefore more dangerous. Cut or rewrite any adjacent line that merely restates 'they liked him' without adding pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 contain no more than one narrator-side 'That was...' or 'It was...' summary opener across those beats, and each moment now lands through specific action, sensation, or social consequence. FAIL if multiple abstract deictic labels remain in those same pivots.
findings.problem: The narration reuses deictic summary stems often enough to become a tic: 'That was the thing,' 'That was the dangerous part too,' 'It was such a small thing,' 'That was part of the problem,' 'It was too exact.' The surrounding prose is more vivid than these labels; repeated abstract throat-clearing slightly flattens the chapter's strongest emotional turns.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least three of these summary openers at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:339, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:383, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:531, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:541 with concrete sensory or social detail that lands the beat directly through Lena's body, the room, or June's face. Keep the interiority, but stop announcing the meaning before the image does the work.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:997-1010 still lands the joke but Gabe's line could not be reassigned to June or Eli without sounding wrong, and the exact phrase 'I contain multitudes' no longer appears in this chapter. Fail if Gabe still speaks in the same mantra line as the two voices the plot later wants linked.
findings.problem: Gabe's exact 'I contain multitudes' repeats June's chapter-1 phrasing and Eli's chapter-2 echo, which collapses idiolect separation in the very chapter meant to legitimize Eli socially. Because the later suspicion clue depends on language overlap feeling specific, this extra reuse makes Gabe sound infected by the same diction instead of like Gabe.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:997-1010, replace Gabe's punch line with a Gabe-specific joke in his own register: trade-worker, family-crowded, slightly gross or overconfident if needed. Keep the comic bounce of the scene, but remove the exact phrase 'I contain multitudes' from Gabe's mouth so June/Eli's overlap stays pointed rather than ambient.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
